1	"""Implements the MultimodalTransformer Model. See https://github.com/yaohungt/Multimodal-Transformer for more."""
2	import math
3	import torch
4	import torch.nn.functional as F
5	from torch import nn
6	
7	
8	class MULTModel(nn.Module):
9	    """
10	    Implements the MultimodalTransformer Model.
11	    
12	    See https://github.com/yaohungt/Multimodal-Transformer for more.
13	    """
14	    
15	    class DefaultHyperParams():
16	        """Set default hyperparameters for the model."""
17	        
18	        num_heads = 3
19	        layers = 3
20	        attn_dropout = 0.1
21	        attn_dropout_modalities = [0.0] * 1000
22	        relu_dropout = 0.1
23	        res_dropout = 0.1
24	        out_dropout = 0.0
25	        embed_dropout = 0.25
26	        embed_dim = 9
27	        attn_mask = True
28	        output_dim = 1
29	        all_steps = False
30	
31	    def __init__(self, n_modalities, n_features, hyp_params=DefaultHyperParams):
32	        """Construct a MulT model."""
33	        super().__init__()
34	        self.n_modalities = n_modalities
35	        self.embed_dim = hyp_params.embed_dim
36	        self.num_heads = hyp_params.num_heads
37	        self.layers = hyp_params.layers
38	        self.attn_dropout = hyp_params.attn_dropout
39	        self.attn_dropout_modalities = hyp_params.attn_dropout_modalities
40	        self.relu_dropout = hyp_params.relu_dropout
41	        self.res_dropout = hyp_params.res_dropout
42	        self.out_dropout = hyp_params.out_dropout
43	        self.embed_dropout = hyp_params.embed_dropout
44	        self.attn_mask = hyp_params.attn_mask
45	        self.all_steps = hyp_params.all_steps
46	
47	        combined_dim = self.embed_dim * n_modalities * n_modalities
48	
49	        # This is actually not a hyperparameter :-)
50	        output_dim = hyp_params.output_dim
51	
52	        # 1. Temporal convolutional layers
53	        self.proj = [nn.Conv1d(n_features[i], self.embed_dim, kernel_size=1,
54	                               padding=0, bias=False) for i in range(n_modalities)]
55	        self.proj = nn.ModuleList(self.proj)
56	
57	        # 2. Crossmodal Attentions
58	        self.trans = [nn.ModuleList([self.get_network(i, j, mem=False) for j in range(
59	            n_modalities)]) for i in range(n_modalities)]
60	        self.trans = nn.ModuleList(self.trans)
61	
62	        # 3. Self Attentions (Could be replaced by LSTMs, GRUs, etc.)
63	        self.trans_mems = [self.get_network(
64	            i, i, mem=True, layers=3) for i in range(n_modalities)]
65	        self.trans_mems = nn.ModuleList(self.trans_mems)
66	
67	        # Projection layers
68	        self.proj1 = nn.Linear(combined_dim, combined_dim)
69	        self.proj2 = nn.Linear(combined_dim, combined_dim)
70	        self.out_layer = nn.Linear(combined_dim, output_dim)
71	
72	    def get_network(self, mod1, mod2, mem, layers=-1):
73	        """Create TransformerEncoder network from layer information."""
74	        if not mem:
75	            embed_dim = self.embed_dim
76	            attn_dropout = self.attn_dropout_modalities[mod2]
77	        else:
78	            embed_dim = self.n_modalities * self.embed_dim
79	            attn_dropout = self.attn_dropout
80	
81	        return TransformerEncoder(embed_dim=embed_dim,
82	                                  num_heads=self.num_heads,
83	                                  layers=max(self.layers, layers),
84	                                  attn_dropout=attn_dropout,
85	                                  relu_dropout=self.relu_dropout,
86	                                  res_dropout=self.res_dropout,
87	                                  embed_dropout=self.embed_dropout,
88	                                  attn_mask=self.attn_mask)
89	
90	    def forward(self, x):
91	        """
92	        Apply MultModel Module to Layer Input.
93	        
94	        Args:
95	            x: layer input. Has size n_modalities * [batch_size, seq_len, n_features]
96	        """
97	        x = [v.permute(0, 2, 1)
98	             for v in x]  # n_modalities * [batch_size, n_features, seq_len]
99	
100	        # Project the textual/visual/audio features
101	        proj_x = [self.proj[i](x[i]) for i in range(self.n_modalities)]
102	        proj_x = torch.stack(proj_x)
103	        # [n_modalities, seq_len, batch_size, proj]
104	        proj_x = proj_x.permute(0, 3, 1, 2)
105	
106	        hs = []
107	        last_hs = []
108	        for i in range(self.n_modalities):
109	            h = []
110	            for j in range(self.n_modalities):
111	                h.append(self.trans[i][j](proj_x[i], proj_x[j], proj_x[j]))
112	            h = torch.cat(h, dim=2)
113	            h = self.trans_mems[i](h)
114	            # if type(h) == tuple:
115	            #     h = h[0]
116	            if self.all_steps:
117	                hs.append(h)
118	            else:
119	                last_hs.append(h[-1])
120	
121	        if self.all_steps:
122	            out = torch.cat(hs, dim=2)  # [seq_len, batch_size, out_features]
123	            out = out.permute(1, 0, 2)  # [batch_size, seq_len, out_features]
124	        else:
125	            out = torch.cat(last_hs, dim=1)
126	
127	        # A residual block
128	        out_proj = self.proj2(
129	            F.dropout(F.relu(self.proj1(out)), p=self.out_dropout, training=self.training))
130	        out_proj += out
131	
132	        out = self.out_layer(out_proj)
133	        return out
134	
135	
136	class TransformerEncoder(nn.Module):
137	    """
138	    Transformer encoder consisting of *args.encoder_layers* layers.
139	    
140	    Each layer is a :class:`TransformerEncoderLayer`.
141	    
142	    Args:
143	        embed_tokens (torch.nn.Embedding): input embedding
144	        num_heads (int): number of heads
145	        layers (int): number of layers
146	        attn_dropout (float): dropout applied on the attention weights
147	        relu_dropout (float): dropout applied on the first layer of the residual block
148	        res_dropout (float): dropout applied on the residual block
149	        attn_mask (bool): whether to apply mask on the attention weights
150	    """
151	
152	    def __init__(self, embed_dim, num_heads, layers, attn_dropout=0.0, relu_dropout=0.0, res_dropout=0.0,
153	                 embed_dropout=0.0, attn_mask=False):
154	        """Initialize Transformer Encoder.
155	
156	        Args:
157	            embed_dim (int): Embedding dimension
158	            num_heads (int): Number of heads
159	            layers (int): Number of layers
160	            attn_dropout (float, optional): Probability of dropout in attention mechanism. Defaults to 0.0.
161	            relu_dropout (float, optional): Probability of dropout after ReLU. Defaults to 0.0.
162	            res_dropout (float, optional): Probability of dropout in residual layer. Defaults to 0.0.
163	            embed_dropout (float, optional): Probability of dropout in embedding layer. Defaults to 0.0.
164	            attn_mask (bool, optional): Whether to apply a mask to the attention or not. Defaults to False.
165	        """
166	        super().__init__()
167	        self.dropout = embed_dropout      # Embedding dropout
168	        self.attn_dropout = attn_dropout
169	        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
170	        self.embed_scale = math.sqrt(embed_dim)
171	        self.embed_positions = SinusoidalPositionalEmbedding(embed_dim)
172	
173	        self.attn_mask = attn_mask
174	
175	        self.layers = nn.ModuleList([])
176	        for _ in range(layers):
177	            new_layer = TransformerEncoderLayer(embed_dim,
178	                                                num_heads=num_heads,
179	                                                attn_dropout=attn_dropout,
180	                                                relu_dropout=relu_dropout,
181	                                                res_dropout=res_dropout,
182	                                                attn_mask=attn_mask)
183	            self.layers.append(new_layer)
184	
185	        self.register_buffer('version', torch.Tensor([2]))
186	        self.normalize = True
187	        if self.normalize:
188	            self.layer_norm = LayerNorm(embed_dim)
189	
190	    def forward(self, x_in, x_in_k=None, x_in_v=None):
191	        """
192	        Apply Transformer Encoder to layer input.
193	        
194	        Args:
195	            x_in (FloatTensor): embedded input of shape `(src_len, batch, embed_dim)`
196	            x_in_k (FloatTensor): embedded input of shape `(src_len, batch, embed_dim)`
197	            x_in_v (FloatTensor): embedded input of shape `(src_len, batch, embed_dim)`
198	        Returns:
199	            dict:
200	                - **encoder_out** (Tensor): the last encoder layer's output of
201	                  shape `(src_len, batch, embed_dim)`
202	                - **encoder_padding_mask** (ByteTensor): the positions of
203	                  padding elements of shape `(batch, src_len)`
204	        """
205	        # embed tokens and positions
206	        x = self.embed_scale * x_in
207	        if self.embed_positions is not None:
208	            # Add positional embedding
209	            x += self.embed_positions(x_in.transpose(0, 1)
210	                                      [:, :, 0]).transpose(0, 1)
211	        x = F.dropout(x, p=self.dropout, training=self.training)
212	
213	        if x_in_k is not None and x_in_v is not None:
214	            # embed tokens and positions
215	            x_k = self.embed_scale * x_in_k
216	            x_v = self.embed_scale * x_in_v
217	            if self.embed_positions is not None:
218	                # Add positional embedding
219	                x_k += self.embed_positions(x_in_k.transpose(0, 1)
220	                                            [:, :, 0]).transpose(0, 1)
221	                # Add positional embedding
222	                x_v += self.embed_positions(x_in_v.transpose(0, 1)
223	                                            [:, :, 0]).transpose(0, 1)
224	            x_k = F.dropout(x_k, p=self.dropout, training=self.training)
225	            x_v = F.dropout(x_v, p=self.dropout, training=self.training)
226	
227	        # encoder layers
228	        intermediates = [x]
229	        for layer in self.layers:
230	            if x_in_k is not None and x_in_v is not None:
231	                x = layer(x, x_k, x_v)
232	            else:
233	                x = layer(x)
234	            intermediates.append(x)
235	
236	        if self.normalize:
237	            x = self.layer_norm(x)
238	
239	        return x
240	
241	
242	class TransformerEncoderLayer(nn.Module):
243	    """Implements encoder layer block.
244	    
245	    In the original paper each operation (multi-head attention or FFN) is
246	    postprocessed with: `dropout -> add residual -> layernorm`. In the
247	    tensor2tensor code they suggest that learning is more robust when
248	    preprocessing each layer with layernorm and postprocessing with:
249	    `dropout -> add residual`. We default to the approach in the paper, but the
250	    tensor2tensor approach can be enabled by setting
251	    *args.encoder_normalize_before* to ``True``.
252	    
253	    """
254	
255	    def __init__(self, embed_dim, num_heads=4, attn_dropout=0.1, relu_dropout=0.1, res_dropout=0.1,
256	                 attn_mask=False):
257	        """Instantiate TransformerEncoderLayer Module.
258	
259	        Args:
260	            embed_dim (int): Embedding dimension
261	            num_heads (int, optional): Number of heads. Defaults to 4.
262	            attn_dropout (float, optional): Dropout for attention mechanism. Defaults to 0.1.
263	            relu_dropout (float, optional): Dropout after ReLU. Defaults to 0.1.
264	            res_dropout (float, optional): Dropout after residual layer. Defaults to 0.1.
265	            attn_mask (bool, optional): Whether to apply an attention mask or not. Defaults to False.
266	        """
267	        super().__init__()
268	        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
269	        self.num_heads = num_heads
270	
271	        self.self_attn = nn.MultiheadAttention(
272	            embed_dim=self.embed_dim,
273	            num_heads=self.num_heads,
274	            dropout=attn_dropout
275	        )
276	        self.attn_mask = attn_mask
277	
278	        self.relu_dropout = relu_dropout
279	        self.res_dropout = res_dropout
280	        self.normalize_before = True
281	
282	        # The "Add & Norm" part in the paper
283	        self.fc1 = Linear(self.embed_dim, 4*self.embed_dim)
284	        self.fc2 = Linear(4*self.embed_dim, self.embed_dim)
285	        self.layer_norms = nn.ModuleList(
286	            [LayerNorm(self.embed_dim) for _ in range(2)])
287	
288	    def forward(self, x, x_k=None, x_v=None):
289	        """
290	        Apply TransformerEncoderLayer to Layer Input.
291	        
292	        Args:
293	            x (Tensor): input to the layer of shape `(seq_len, batch, embed_dim)`
294	            encoder_padding_mask (ByteTensor): binary ByteTensor of shape
295	                `(batch, src_len)` where padding elements are indicated by ``1``.
296	            x_k (Tensor): same as x
297	            x_v (Tensor): same as x
298	        Returns:
299	            encoded output of shape `(batch, src_len, embed_dim)`
300	        """
301	        residual = x
302	        x = self._maybe_layer_norm(0, x, before=True)
303	        mask = buffered_future_mask(x, x_k) if self.attn_mask else None
304	        if x_k is None and x_v is None:
305	            x, _ = self.self_attn(query=x, key=x, value=x, attn_mask=mask)
306	        else:
307	            x_k = self._maybe_layer_norm(0, x_k, before=True)
308	            x_v = self._maybe_layer_norm(0, x_v, before=True)
309	            x, _ = self.self_attn(query=x, key=x_k, value=x_v, attn_mask=mask)
310	        x = F.dropout(x, p=self.res_dropout, training=self.training)
311	        x = residual + x
312	        x = self._maybe_layer_norm(0, x, after=True)
313	
314	        residual = x
315	        x = self._maybe_layer_norm(1, x, before=True)
316	        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))
317	        x = F.dropout(x, p=self.relu_dropout, training=self.training)
318	        x = self.fc2(x)
319	        x = F.dropout(x, p=self.res_dropout, training=self.training)
320	        x = residual + x
321	        x = self._maybe_layer_norm(1, x, after=True)
322	        return x
323	
324	    def _maybe_layer_norm(self, i, x, before=False, after=False):
325	        assert before ^ after
326	        if after ^ self.normalize_before:
327	            return self.layer_norms[i](x)
328	        else:
329	            return x
330	
331	
332	def fill_with_neg_inf(t):
333	    """FP16-compatible function that fills a tensor with -inf."""
334	    return t.float().fill_(float('-inf')).type_as(t)
335	
336	
337	def buffered_future_mask(tensor, tensor2=None):
338	    """Generate buffered future mask.
339	
340	    Args:
341	        tensor (torch.Tensor): Tensor to initialize mask from.
342	        tensor2 (torch.Tensor, optional): Tensor to initialize target mask from. Defaults to None.
343	
344	    Returns:
345	        torch.Tensor: Buffered future mask.
346	    """
347	    dim1 = dim2 = tensor.size(0)
348	    if tensor2 is not None:
349	        dim2 = tensor2.size(0)
350	    future_mask = torch.triu(fill_with_neg_inf(
351	        torch.ones(dim1, dim2)), 1+abs(dim2-dim1))
352	    if tensor.is_cuda:
353	        future_mask = future_mask.to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
354	    return future_mask[:dim1, :dim2]
355	
356	
357	def Linear(in_features, out_features, bias=True):
358	    """Generate Linear Layer with given parameters and Xavier initialization.
359	
360	    Args:
361	        in_features (int): Number of input features
362	        out_features (int): Number of output features
363	        bias (bool, optional): Whether to include a bias term or not. Defaults to True.
364	
365	    Returns:
366	        nn.Module: Initialized Linear Module.
367	    """
368	    m = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features, bias)
369	    nn.init.xavier_uniform_(m.weight)
370	    if bias:
371	        nn.init.constant_(m.bias, 0.)
372	    return m
373	
374	
375	def LayerNorm(embedding_dim):
376	    """Generate LayerNorm Layer with given parameters.
377	
378	    Args:
379	        embedding_dim (int): Embedding dimension
380	
381	    Returns:
382	        nn.Module: Initialized LayerNorm Module
383	    """
384	    m = nn.LayerNorm(embedding_dim)
385	    return m
386	
387	
388	"""Implements Positional Encoding.
389	
390	Adapted from fairseq repo.
391	"""
392	def make_positions(tensor, padding_idx, left_pad):
393	    """Replace non-padding symbols with their position numbers.
394	    
395	    Position numbers begin at padding_idx+1.
396	    Padding symbols are ignored, but it is necessary to specify whether padding
397	    is added on the left side (left_pad=True) or right side (left_pad=False).
398	    
399	    Args:
400	        tensor (torch.Tensor): Tensor to generate padding on.   
401	        padding_idx (int): Position numbers start at padding_idx + 1
402	        left_pad (bool): Whether to pad from the left or from the right.
403	
404	    Returns:
405	        torch.Tensor: Padded output
406	    """
407	    max_pos = padding_idx + 1 + tensor.size(1)
408	    device = tensor.get_device()
409	    buf_name = f'range_buf_{device}'
410	    if not hasattr(make_positions, buf_name):
411	        setattr(make_positions, buf_name, tensor.new())
412	    setattr(make_positions, buf_name, getattr(
413	        make_positions, buf_name).type_as(tensor))
414	    if getattr(make_positions, buf_name).numel() < max_pos:
415	        torch.arange(padding_idx + 1, max_pos,
416	                     out=getattr(make_positions, buf_name))
417	    mask = tensor.ne(padding_idx)
418	    positions = getattr(make_positions, buf_name)[
419	        :tensor.size(1)].expand_as(tensor)
420	    if left_pad:
421	        positions = positions - \
422	            mask.size(1) + mask.long().sum(dim=1).unsqueeze(1)
423	    new_tensor = tensor.clone()
424	    return new_tensor.masked_scatter_(mask, positions[mask]).long()
425	
426	
427	class SinusoidalPositionalEmbedding(nn.Module):
428	    """
429	    This module produces sinusoidal positional embeddings of any length.
430	    
431	    Padding symbols are ignored, but it is necessary to specify whether padding
432	    is added on the left side (left_pad=True) or right side (left_pad=False).
433	    """
434	
435	    def __init__(self, embedding_dim, padding_idx=0, left_pad=0):
436	        """Instantiate SinusoidalPositionalEmbedding Module.
437	
438	        Args:
439	            embedding_dim (int): Embedding dimension
440	            padding_idx (int, optional): Padding index. Defaults to 0.
441	            left_pad (int, optional): Whether to pad from the left or not. Defaults to 0.
442	        """
443	        super().__init__()
444	        self.embedding_dim = embedding_dim
445	        self.padding_idx = padding_idx
446	        self.left_pad = left_pad
447	        # device --> actual weight; due to nn.DataParallel :-(
448	        self.weights = dict()
449	        self.register_buffer('_float_tensor', torch.FloatTensor(1))
450	
451	    @staticmethod
452	    def get_embedding(num_embeddings, embedding_dim, padding_idx=None):
453	        """Build sinusoidal embeddings.
454	        
455	        This matches the implementation in tensor2tensor, but differs slightly
456	        from the description in Section 3.5 of "Attention Is All You Need".
457	        """
458	        half_dim = embedding_dim // 2
459	        emb = math.log(10000) / (half_dim - 1)
460	        emb = torch.exp(torch.arange(half_dim, dtype=torch.float) * -emb)
461	        emb = torch.arange(num_embeddings, dtype=torch.float).unsqueeze(
462	            1) * emb.unsqueeze(0)
463	        emb = torch.cat([torch.sin(emb), torch.cos(emb)],
464	                        dim=1).view(num_embeddings, -1)
465	        if embedding_dim % 2 == 1:
466	            # zero pad
467	            emb = torch.cat([emb, torch.zeros(num_embeddings, 1)], dim=1)
468	        if padding_idx is not None:
469	            emb[padding_idx, :] = 0
470	        return emb
471	
472	    def forward(self, input):
473	        """Apply PositionalEncodings to Input.
474	        
475	        Input is expected to be of size [bsz x seqlen].
476	
477	        Args:
478	            input (torch.Tensor): Layer input
479	
480	        Returns:
481	            torch.Tensor: Layer output
482	        """
483	        bsz, seq_len = input.size()
484	        max_pos = self.padding_idx + 1 + seq_len
485	        device = input.get_device()
486	        if device not in self.weights or max_pos > self.weights[device].size(0):
487	            # recompute/expand embeddings if needed
488	            self.weights[device] = SinusoidalPositionalEmbedding.get_embedding(
489	                max_pos,
490	                self.embedding_dim,
491	                self.padding_idx,
492	            )
493	        self.weights[device] = self.weights[device].type_as(self._float_tensor)
494	        positions = make_positions(input, self.padding_idx, self.left_pad)
495	        return self.weights[device].index_select(0, positions.reshape(-1)).reshape((bsz, seq_len, -1)).detach()
496
```
</details></tool-use>

1	"""Implements sensor fusion networks for supervised and self-supervised objectives."""
2	import torch
3	import torch.nn as nn
4	from torch.distributions import Normal
5	
6	
7	class SensorFusion(nn.Module): #pragma: no cover
8	    """
9	    Implements traditional SensorFusionNetwork.
10	    
11	    #
12	        Regular SensorFusionNetwork Architecture
13	        Number of parameters:
14	        Inputs:
15	            image:   batch_size x 3 x 128 x 128
16	            force:   batch_size x 6 x 32
17	            proprio: batch_size x 8
18	            action:  batch_size x action_dim
19	    """
20	    
21	    def __init__(
22	        self, device, z_dim=128, action_dim=4, encoder=False, deterministic=False
23	    ):
24	        """Initialize SensorFusionNetwork.
25	
26	        Args:
27	            device (torch.device): Device to train/test model on.
28	            z_dim (int, optional): Z dimension size. Defaults to 128.
29	            action_dim (int, optional): Action dimension size. Defaults to 4.
30	            encoder (bool, optional): Whether to apply action encoder or not. Defaults to False.
31	            deterministic (bool, optional): Whether the fusion networks is deterministic or not. Defaults to False.
32	        """
33	        super().__init__()
34	
35	        self.z_dim = z_dim
36	        self.encoder_bool = encoder
37	        self.device = device
38	        self.deterministic = deterministic
39	
40	        # zero centered, 1 std normal distribution
41	        self.z_prior_m = torch.nn.Parameter(
42	            torch.zeros(1, self.z_dim), requires_grad=False
43	        )
44	        self.z_prior_v = torch.nn.Parameter(
45	            torch.ones(1, self.z_dim), requires_grad=False
46	        )
47	        self.z_prior = (self.z_prior_m, self.z_prior_v)
48	
49	        # -----------------------
50	        # action fusion network
51	        # -----------------------
52	        self.st_fusion_fc1 = nn.Sequential(
53	            nn.Linear(32 + self.z_dim, 128), nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True)
54	        )
55	        self.st_fusion_fc2 = nn.Sequential(
56	            nn.Linear(128, self.z_dim), nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True)
57	        )
58	
59	        if deterministic:
60	            # -----------------------
61	            # modality fusion network
62	            # -----------------------
63	            # 4 Total modalities each (2 * z_dim)
64	            self.fusion_fc1 = nn.Sequential(
65	                nn.Linear(4 * 2 * self.z_dim,
66	                          128), nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True)
67	            )
68	            self.fusion_fc2 = nn.Sequential(
69	                nn.Linear(self.z_dim, self.z_dim), nn.LeakyReLU(
70	                    0.1, inplace=True)
71	            )
72	
73	        # -----------------------
74	        # weight initialization
75	        # -----------------------
76	        for m in self.modules():
77	            if isinstance(m, nn.Conv2d) or isinstance(m, nn.ConvTranspose2d):
78	                nn.init.kaiming_normal_(m.weight.data)
79	                if m.bias is not None:
80	                    m.bias.data.zero_()
81	            elif isinstance(m, nn.BatchNorm2d):
82	                m.weight.data.fill_(1)
83	                m.bias.data.zero_()
84	
85	    def forward_encoder(self, img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded):
86	        """Encode output using forward encoder.
87	
88	        Args:
89	            img_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded image.
90	            frc_encoded (torch.Tesnor): Encoded force.
91	            proprio_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded proprioception
92	            depth_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded depth.
93	            action_encoded (torch.Tensor): Encoded action.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            torch.Tensor: tuple of outputs
97	        """
98	        # Get encoded outputs
99	        img_out, img_out_convs = img_encoded
100	        depth_out, depth_out_convs = depth_encoded
101	        frc_out = frc_encoded
102	        proprio_out = proprio_encoded
103	
104	        # batch size
105	        batch_dim = img_out.size()[0]
106	
107	        if self.deterministic:
108	            # multimodal embedding
109	            mm_f1 = torch.cat(
110	                [img_out, frc_out, proprio_out, depth_out], 1).squeeze()
111	            mm_f2 = self.fusion_fc1(mm_f1)
112	            z = self.fusion_fc2(mm_f2)
113	
114	        else:
115	            # Encoder priors
116	            mu_prior, var_prior = self.z_prior
117	
118	            # Duplicate prior parameters for each data point in the batch
119	            mu_prior_resized = duplicate(mu_prior, batch_dim).unsqueeze(2)
120	            var_prior_resized = duplicate(var_prior, batch_dim).unsqueeze(2)
121	
122	            # Modality Mean and Variances
123	            mu_z_img, var_z_img = gaussian_parameters(img_out, dim=1)
124	            mu_z_frc, var_z_frc = gaussian_parameters(frc_out, dim=1)
125	            mu_z_proprio, var_z_proprio = gaussian_parameters(
126	                proprio_out, dim=1)
127	            mu_z_depth, var_z_depth = gaussian_parameters(depth_out, dim=1)
128	
129	            # Tile distribution parameters using concatonation
130	            m_vect = torch.cat(
131	                [mu_z_img, mu_z_frc, mu_z_proprio,
132	                    mu_z_depth, mu_prior_resized], dim=2
133	            )
134	            var_vect = torch.cat(
135	                [var_z_img, var_z_frc, var_z_proprio,
136	                    var_z_depth, var_prior_resized],
137	                dim=2,
138	            )
139	
140	            # Fuse modalities mean / variances using product of experts
141	            mu_z, var_z = product_of_experts(m_vect, var_vect)
142	
143	            # Sample Gaussian to get latent
144	            z = sample_gaussian(mu_z, var_z, self.device)
145	
146	        if self.encoder_bool or action_encoded is None:
147	            if self.deterministic:
148	                return img_out, frc_out, proprio_out, depth_out, z
149	            else:
150	                return img_out_convs, img_out, frc_out, proprio_out, depth_out, z
151	        else:
152	            # action embedding
153	            act_feat = action_encoded
154	
155	            # state-action feature
156	            mm_act_f1 = torch.cat([z, act_feat], 1)
157	            mm_act_f2 = self.st_fusion_fc1(mm_act_f1)
158	            mm_act_feat = self.st_fusion_fc2(mm_act_f2)
159	
160	            if self.deterministic:
161	                return img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z
162	            else:
163	                return img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z, mu_z, var_z, mu_prior, var_prior
164	
165	    def weight_parameters(self):
166	        """Get weight parameters."""
167	        return [param for name, param in self.named_parameters() if "weight" in name]
168	
169	    def bias_parameters(self):
170	        """Get bias parameters."""
171	        return [param for name, param in self.named_parameters() if "bias" in name]

174	class SensorFusionSelfSupervised(SensorFusion):
175	    """
176	    Implements SensorFusionNetwork for Self-Supervision.
177	    
178	        Regular SensorFusionNetwork Architecture
179	        Inputs:
180	            image:   batch_size x 3 x 128 x 128
181	            force:   batch_size x 6 x 32
182	            proprio: batch_size x 8
183	            action:  batch_size x action_dim
184	    """
185	
186	    def __init__(
187	        self, device, z_dim=128, encoder=False, deterministic=False
188	    ):
189	        """Initialize SensorFusionSelfSupervised Module.
190	
191	        Args:
192	            device (torch.Device): Device to train/test on.
193	            z_dim (int, optional): Z dimension size for encoders. Defaults to 128.
194	            encoder (bool, optional): Whether to apply the encoders or not. Defaults to False.
195	            deterministic (bool, optional): Whether the fusion network is deterministic or not. Defaults to False.
196	        """
197	        super().__init__(device, z_dim, encoder, deterministic)
198	
199	        self.deterministic = deterministic
200	
201	    def forward(self, input):
202	        """Apply SensorFusionSelfSupervised Module to Layer Input.
203	
204	        Args:
205	            input (torch.Tensor): Layer Input
206	
207	        Returns:
208	            torch.Tensor: Layer Output
209	        """
210	        img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded = input
211	
212	        if self.encoder_bool:
213	            # returning latent space representation if model is set in encoder mode
214	            z = self.forward_encoder(
215	                img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded)
216	            return z
217	
218	        elif action_encoded is None:
219	            z = self.forward_encoder(
220	                img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, None)
221	            pair_out = self.pair_fc(z)

223	
224	        else:
225	            if self.deterministic:
226	                img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z = self.forward_encoder(
227	                    img_encoded, frc_encoded, proprio_encoded, depth_encoded, action_encoded
228	                )
229	            else:
230	                img_out_convs, mm_act_feat, z, mu_z, var_z, mu_prior, var_prior = self.forward_encoder(
231	                    img_encoded,
232	                    frc_encoded,
233	                    proprio_encoded,
234	                    depth_encoded,
235	                    action_encoded,
236	                )
237	
238	        # ---------------- Training Objectives ----------------
239	
240	        # tile state-action features and append to conv map
241	        batch_dim = mm_act_feat.size(0)  # batch size
242	        tiled_feat = mm_act_feat.view(
243	            batch_dim, self.z_dim, 1, 1).expand(-1, -1, 2, 2)
244	
245	        if self.deterministic:
246	            return z, mm_act_feat, tiled_feat, img_out_convs
247	        else:
248	            return z, mm_act_feat, tiled_feat, img_out_convs, mu_z, var_z, mu_prior, var_prior

251	class roboticsConcat(nn.Module):
252	    """Concatenates tensors for robotics fusion."""
253	    
254	    def __init__(self, name=None):
255	        """Initialize roboticsConcat module.
256	
257	        Args:
258	            name (str, optional): What kind of concatenation to do. Can be "noconcat", "image" or "simple". Defaults to None.
259	        """
260	        super(roboticsConcat, self).__init__()
261	        self.name = name
262	
263	    def forward(self, x):
264	        """Apply roboticsConcat module to Layer Input.
265	
266	        Args:
267	            x (torch.Tensor): Layer Input
268	
269	        Returns:
270	            torch.Tensor: Layer output.
271	        """
272	        if self.name == "noconcat":
273	            return [x[0][0].squeeze(), x[1].squeeze(), x[2].squeeze(), x[3][0].squeeze(), x[4]]
274	        if self.name == "image":
275	            return torch.cat([x[0][0].squeeze(), x[1][0].squeeze(), x[2]], 1)
276	        if self.name == "simple":
277	            return torch.cat([x[0].squeeze(), x[1]], 1)
278	        return torch.cat([x[0][0].squeeze(), x[1].squeeze(), x[2].squeeze(), x[3][0].squeeze(), x[4]], 1)
279	    
280	
281	def sample_gaussian(m, v, device):
282	    """Sample from Gaussian Distribution with known mean and variance.
283	
284	    Args:
285	        m (float): Mean of sampled Gaussian
286	        v (float): Variance of sampled Gaussian
287	        device (torch.device): Device on which to create tensor.
288	
289	    Returns:
290	        torch.Tensor: Sampled Gaussian Tensor
291	    """
292	    epsilon = Normal(0, 1).sample(m.size())
293	    z = m + torch.sqrt(v) * epsilon.to(device)
294	
295	    return z
296	
297	
298	def gaussian_parameters(h, dim=-1):
299	    """Return parameters for gaussian distribution given vector for robotics."""
300	    m, h = torch.split(h, h.size(dim) // 2, dim=dim)
301	    v = F.softplus(h) + 1e-8
302	    return m, v
303	
304	
305	def product_of_experts(m_vect, v_vect):
306	    """Compute product of experts."""
307	    T_vect = 1.0 / v_vect
308	
309	    mu = (m_vect * T_vect).sum(2) * (1 / T_vect.sum(2))
310	    var = 1 / T_vect.sum(2)
311	
312	    return mu, var
313	
314	
315	def duplicate(x, rep):
316	    """Duplicate x rep times.
317	
318	    Args:
319	        x (torch.Tensor): Tensor to replicate
320	        rep (int): Number of times to replicate
321	
322	    Returns:
323	        torch.Tensor: Replicated Tensor
324	    """
325	    return x.expand(rep, *x.shape).reshape(-1, *x.shape[1:])
326	
327	
328	def depth_deconv(in_planes, out_planes):
329	    """Instantiate depth deconvolution module.
330	
331	    Args:
332	        in_planes (int): Number of input channels
333	        out_planes (int): Number of output channels
334	
335	    Returns:
336	        nn.Module: Depth Deconvolution Module
337	    """
338	    return nn.Sequential(
339	        nn.Conv2d(
340	            in_planes, 16, kernel_size=3, stride=1, padding=(3 - 1) // 2, bias=True
341	        ),
342	        nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True),
343	        nn.Conv2d(16, 16, kernel_size=3, stride=1,
344	                  padding=(3 - 1) // 2, bias=True),
345	        nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True),
346	        nn.ConvTranspose2d(
347	            16, out_planes, kernel_size=4, stride=2, padding=1, bias=True
348	        ),
349	        nn.LeakyReLU(0.1, inplace=True),
350	    )
351

12	criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()

16	def getloss(model, head, data, monum, batch_size):
17	    """Get loss for model given classification head.
18	
19	    Args:
20	        model (nn.Module): Module to evaluate
21	        head (nn.Module): Classification head.
22	        data (torch.utils.data.Dataloader): Dataloader to evaluate on.
23	        monum (int): Unimodal model index.
24	        batch_size (int): (unused) Batch Size
25	
26	    Returns:
27	        float: Average loss per sample.
28	    """
29	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
30	    losses = 0.0
31	    total = 0
32	    with torch.inference_mode():
33	        for j in data:
34	            total += len(j[0])
35	            train_x = j[monum].float().to(device)
36	            train_y = j[-1].to(device)
37	            out = model(train_x)
38	            # if (monum==1):
39	            
40	            out = head(out)
41	            loss = criterion(out, train_y.squeeze())
42	            
43	            losses += loss*len(j[0])
44	    return losses/total
45	
46	
47	def train_unimodal(model, head, optim, trains, valids, monum, epoch, batch_size):
48	    """Train unimodal gradient blending module.
49	
50	    Args:
51	        model (nn.Module): Unimodal encoder
52	        head (nn.Module): Classifier instance
53	        optim (torch.optim.Optimizer): Optimizer instance
54	        trains (torch.utils.data.DataLoader):  Training Dataloader Instance
55	        valids (torch.utils.data.DataLoader):  Validation DataLoader Instance
56	        monum (int): Modality index
57	        epoch (int): Number of epochs to train on
58	        batch_size (int): Batch size of data loaders
59	
60	    Returns:
61	        float: Metric
62	    """
63	    device = torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
64	    ltN = getloss(model, head, trains, monum, batch_size)
65	    lvN = getloss(model, head, valids, monum, batch_size)
66	    for i in range(epoch):
67	        totalloss = 0.0
68	        total = 0
69	        for j in trains:
70	            total += len(j[0])
71	            train_x = j[monum].float().to(device)
72	            train_y = j[-1].to(device)
73	            optim.zero_grad()
74	            out = model(train_x)
75	            out = head(out)
76	            loss = criterion(out, train_y.squeeze())
77	            totalloss += loss.item() * len(j[0])
78	            loss.backward()
79	            optim.step()
80	        print("Epoch "+str(i)+" loss: "+str(totalloss / total))
81	
82	    ltNn = getloss(model, head, trains, monum, batch_size)
83	    lvNn = getloss(model, head, valids, monum, batch_size)
84	    print("Final train loss: "+str(ltNn)+" valid loss: "+str(lvNn))
85	    oNn = lvNn-ltNn
86	    oN = lvN-ltN
87	    if delta:
88	        oi = oNn-oN
89	        g = lvNn-lvN
90	    else:
91	        oi = oNn
92	        if oi < 0:
93	            oi = 0.0001
94	        g = lvNn
95	    print("raw: "+str(g/(oi*oi)))
96	    return abs(g/(oi*oi))

25	    See https://github.com/slyviacassell/_MFAS/blob/master/models/searchable.py for more details.

38	        search_iter (int, optional): Number of iterations to search with MFAS. Defaults to 3.
39	        num_samples (int, optional): Sample number. Defaults to 15.

76	def sample_k_configurations(configurations, accuracies_, k, temperature):
77	    """Sample k configurations from list of configurations and accuracies, based on past performance.
78	
79	    Args:
80	        configurations (list): List of configurations.
81	        accuracies_ (list): List of accuracies for each config.
82	        k (int): Number of configurations to sample on.
83	        temperature (float): Temperature for the sample distribution.
84	
85	    Returns:
86	        list: List of sampled configurations.
87	    """
88	    accuracies = np.array(accuracies_)
89	    p = accuracies / accuracies.sum()
90	    powered = pow(p, 1.0 / temperature)
91	    p = powered / powered.sum()
92	
93	    indices = np.random.choice(len(configurations), k, replace=False, p=p)
94	    samples = [configurations[i] for i in indices]
95	
96	    return samples

Based on my comprehensive analysis of the Python codebase, I've identified numerous performance bottlenecks, memory issues, and bugs. Let me compile my findings:

## COMPREHENSIVE CODE ANALYSIS REPORT

### CRITICAL ISSUES FOUND

**1. Device Hardcoding - Device Mismatch Bug**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/fusions/MVAE.py`, line 102-104
- Issue type: Bug
- Problem: `_prior_expert()` function hardcodes device placement but doesn't check if inputs are on that device. This causes dimension mismatches when tensors are on different devices.
- Suggested fix: Accept device as parameter or retrieve from input tensors

**2. Missing torch.no_grad() in Inference**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/Supervised_Learning.py`, line 274
- Issue type: Memory / Performance
- Problem: `single_test()` uses `torch.inference_mode()` but validation loop in `train()` at line 170 uses `torch.no_grad()` inconsistently. The use of `inference_mode()` is better but mixing creates confusion.
- Suggested fix: Use `torch.inference_mode()` consistently throughout

**3. Inefficient Concatenation Loop in MVAE**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/fusions/MVAE.py`, lines 38-41
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: `ProductOfExperts.forward()` concatenates tensors in a loop using `torch.cat()` repeatedly. This creates unnecessary intermediate tensors.
- Suggested fix: Collect all tensors in a list first, then concatenate once: `torch.cat([mu, *mus], dim=0)`

**4. Redundant Matrix Operations in CCA Loss**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/cca.py`, lines 80-84
- Issue type: Performance / Redundancy
- Problem: Computing `SigmaHat11RootInv` and `SigmaHat22RootInv` uses three matrix operations each (matmul, diag, matmul). These could be optimized using torch.linalg.solve.
- Suggested fix: Replace with `torch.linalg.solve(SigmaHat11, identity)` pattern

**5. Nested Loop in MultiSimilarityLoss**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/contrast.py`, lines 290-321
- Issue type: Performance - O(n²) complexity
- Problem: Double nested loop over batch_size and label dimensions without vectorization causes O(n²) complexity.
- Suggested fix: Vectorize using torch operations: `sim_mat` is already computed, use broadcasting instead of explicit loops

**6. Device String Hardcoding Throughout**
- Multiple files: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/objectives_for_supervised_learning.py` (lines 15, 42, 44, 85, 87, 139, 140, 151, 171), `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/Supervised_Learning.py` (multiple lines)
- Issue type: Bug / Design
- Problem: Hard-coded device placement `torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")` appears ~50+ times across codebase. Should be centralized and passed as parameter.
- Suggested fix: Create a global device registry or pass device to functions consistently

**7. Memory Leak in MCTN - Variable Accumulation**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/fusions/MCTN.py`, lines 191-216
- Issue type: Memory
- Problem: `Variable()` wrapper and repeated tensor creation in loop without proper cleanup. PyTorch 1.0+ doesn't need `Variable()`.
- Suggested fix: Remove `Variable()` wrappers and use modern PyTorch syntax

**8. Inefficient Normalization in Perturbation**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/regularization.py`, line 18
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: `_add_noise_to_tensor()` computes `std()` which can trigger a full tensor scan. Called repeatedly in loops.
- Suggested fix: Cache std computation or use in-place operations where possible

**9. List Comprehension Inefficiency in SearchTools**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/utils/search_tools.py`, lines 36-37
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: Iterating through configurations one-by-one for surrogate prediction. Should batch predict.
- Suggested fix: Implement batch prediction in surrogate model

**10. Repeated .item() Calls in Loops**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/utils/surrogate.py`, line 145
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: Converting tensors to Python scalars inside loops breaks vectorization.
- Suggested fix: Convert once after computation completes

**11. Off-by-One Risk in Loss Aggregation**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/Supervised_Learning.py`, line 160
- Issue type: Bug
- Problem: `loss.item() * len(j[-1])` assumes last element is labels but could fail if batch structure changes.
- Suggested fix: Verify batch structure at initialization, add assertions

**12. Dimension Mismatch in Tensor Operations**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/fusions/common_fusions.py`, lines 213-220
- Issue type: Bug - Silent dimension mismatch
- Problem: `MultiplicativeInteractions2Modal` uses einsum operations that don't validate input dimensions match expected shapes.
- Suggested fix: Add shape assertions at the start of forward pass

**13. Inefficient Softmax in Evaluation**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/Supervised_Learning.py`, line 199
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: Computing softmax multiple times for AUPRC calculation. Softmax is expensive.
- Suggested fix: Compute once and reuse

**14. Missing .detach() Before NumPy Conversion**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/eval_scripts/performance.py`, line 28
- Issue type: Memory
- Problem: Converting computation graph tensors to numpy without detach can retain gradients.
- Suggested fix: Add `.detach()` before `.cpu().numpy()`

**15. Inplace Operation on View - Bug Risk**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/contrast.py`, line 25
- Issue type: Bug
- Problem: `probs.div_(probs.sum())` uses inplace on tensor that might be a view or require gradients.
- Suggested fix: Use non-inplace: `probs / probs.sum()`

**16. Large Tensors Not Freed in Loops**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/datasets/mimic/get_data.py`, lines 90-99
- Issue type: Memory
- Problem: `copy.deepcopy(datasets[...])` in loop creates large copies without explicit deletion.
- Suggested fix: Use context managers or explicit `del` statements

**17. Redundant Normalization Computation**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/contrast.py`, lines 227-228
- Issue type: Performance / Redundancy
- Problem: Computing `P_neg.clone().fill_(...)` is inefficient - creates clone then fills. Should use full() instead.
- Suggested fix: `torch.full_like(P_neg, m * Pn)` is faster

**18. Missing torch.no_grad() in Loss Functions**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/cca.py`, lines 37-106
- Issue type: Memory
- Problem: Complex matrix operations build computation graphs even when only loss is needed.
- Suggested fix: Wrap forward pass in `torch.no_grad()` for inference

**19. Inefficient Repeat Operations**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/fusions/common_fusions.py`, line 188
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: `_repeatHorizontally()` uses repeat + view + transpose which is inefficient.
- Suggested fix: Use `expand()` which doesn't allocate memory, or reshape directly

**20. Redundant Float Conversion**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/objective_functions/recon.py`, lines 48, 49
- Issue type: Performance / Redundancy
- Problem: tensors converted to float multiple times via operations.
- Suggested fix: Ensure dtype is correct at input rather than repeated conversions

**21. Inefficient Positional Embedding Caching**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/fusions/mult.py`, lines 408-424
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: Using `setattr()` as dynamic caching mechanism is slow. Should use dict or proper cache.
- Suggested fix: Implement proper caching with standard Python dict

**22. Gradient Accumulation Without Zeroing Risk**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/gradient_blend.py`, line 73
- Issue type: Bug - gradient accumulation
- Problem: `optim.zero_grad()` happens but if exception occurs mid-loop, next iteration may double-count.
- Suggested fix: Use try-finally blocks or context managers

**23. Inconsistent Batch Size Handling**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/Supervised_Learning.py`, lines 144-150
- Issue type: Bug - silent dimension mismatch
- Problem: Packed vs unpacked input handling not validated - could cause shape errors silently.
- Suggested fix: Add shape validation before processing

**24. Missing CUDA Synchronization**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/eval_scripts/complexity.py` (referenced in training)
- Issue type: Performance
- Problem: No `torch.cuda.synchronize()` before timing measurements, causing inaccurate benchmarks.
- Suggested fix: Add synchronization before time measurements for accuracy timing

**25. Inefficient String Concatenation**
- File: `/home/bagus/github/multibench/training_structures/Supervised_Learning.py`, lines 165, 209-210
- Issue type: Performance (minor)
- Problem: String concatenation in loops with `+` operator is inefficient.
- Suggested fix: Use f-strings or `str.join()`

This codebase has substantial performance optimization opportunities, particularly in:
- Eliminating redundant device string handling
- Vectorizing nested loops (especially in loss functions)
- Implementing proper gradient checkpointing for memory-intensive modules
- Caching expensive computations
- Using modern PyTorch patterns (no Variable, proper grad handling)

 24 │         self.size = size
 25 │ 
 26 │     def forward(self, mus, logvars, eps=1e-8):
 27 │         """Apply Product of Experts Layer.
 28 │ 
 29 │         Args:
 30 │             mus (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Mus
 31 │             logvars (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Logvars
 32 │             eps (float, optional): Epsilon for log-exponent trick. Defaults to 1e-8.
 33 │ 
 34 │         Returns:
 35 │             torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor: Output of PoE layer.
 36 │         """
 37 │         mu, logvar = _prior_expert(self.size, len(mus[0]))
 38 │         for i in range(len(mus)):
 39 │             
 40 │             mu = torch.cat((mu, mus[i].unsqueeze(0)), dim=0)
 41 │             logvar = torch.cat((logvar, logvars[i].unsqueeze(0)), dim=0)
 42 │ 
 43 │         var = torch.exp(logvar) + eps
 44 │         # precision of i-th Gaussian expert at point x
 45 │         T = 1. / var
 46 │         pd_mu = torch.sum(mu * T, dim=0) / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
 47 │         pd_var = 1. / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
 48 │         pd_logvar = torch.log(pd_var)
 49 │         return pd_mu, pd_logvar
 50 │ 
 51 │ 
 52 │ class ProductOfExperts_Zipped(nn.Module):
 53 │     """
 54 │     Return parameters for product of independent experts.
 55 │     
 56 │     See https://arxiv.org/pdf/1410.7827.pdf for equations.
 57 │     """
 58 │ 
 59 │     def __init__(self, size):
 60 │         """Initialize Product of Experts Object.
 61 │ 
 62 │         Args:
 63 │             size (tuple): Size of Product of Experts Layer
 64 │         
 65 │         """
 66 │         super(ProductOfExperts_Zipped, self).__init__()
 67 │         self.size = size
 68 │ 
 69 │     def forward(self, zipped, eps=1e-8):
 70 │         """Apply Product of Experts Layer.
 71 │ 
 72 │         Args:
 73 │             mus (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Mus
 74 │             logvars (torch.Tensor): torch.Tensor of Logvars
 75 │             eps (float, optional): Epsilon for log-exponent trick. Defaults to 1e-8.
 76 │ 
 77 │         Returns:
 78 │             torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor: Output of PoE layer.
 79 │         """
 80 │         mus = [i[0] for i in zipped]
 81 │         logvars = [i[1] for i in zipped]
 82 │         mu, logvar = _prior_expert(self.size, len(mus[0]))
 83 │         for i in range(len(mus)):
 84 │             mu = torch.cat((mu, mus[i].unsqueeze(0)), dim=0)
 85 │             logvar = torch.cat((logvar, logvars[i].unsqueeze(0)), dim=0)
 86 │ 
 87 │         var = torch.exp(logvar) + eps
 88 │         T = 1. / var
 89 │         pd_mu = torch.sum(mu * T, dim=0) / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
 90 │         pd_var = 1. / torch.sum(T, dim=0)
 91 │         pd_logvar = torch.log(pd_var)
 92 │         return pd_mu, pd_logvar
 93 │ 
 94 │ 
 95 │ def _prior_expert(size, batch_size):
 96 │     """
 97 │     Universal prior expert. Here we use a spherical.
 98 │     
 99 │     Gaussian: N(0, 1)
100 │     """
101 │     size = (size[0], batch_size, size[2])
102 │     mu = torch.zeros(size)
103 │     logvar = torch.log(torch.ones(size))
104 │     return mu.to(device), logvar.to(device)
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
  1 │ """Implement objectives for contrastive loss."""
  2 │ import torch
  3 │ from torch import nn
  4 │ import math
  5 │ 
  6 │ 
  7 │ eps = 1e-7
  8 │ 
  9 │ 
 10 │ class AliasMethod(object):
 11 │     """
 12 │     Initializes a generic method to sample from arbritrary discrete probability methods.
 13 │     
 14 │     Sourced From https://hips.seas.harvard.edu/blog/2013/03/03/the-alias-method-efficient-sampling-with-many-discrete-outcomes/.
 15 │     Alternatively, look here for more details: http://cgi.cs.mcgill.ca/~enewel3/posts/alias-method/index.html.
 16 │     """
 17 │ 
 18 │     def __init__(self, probs):
 19 │         """Initialize AliasMethod object.
 20 │ 
 21 │         Args:
 22 │             probs (list[int]): List of probabilities for each object. Can be greater than 1, but will be normalized.
 23 │         """
 24 │         if probs.sum() > 1:
 25 │             probs.div_(probs.sum())
 26 │         K = len(probs)
 27 │         self.prob = torch.zeros(K)
 28 │         self.alias = torch.LongTensor([0]*K)
 29 │ 
 30 │         # Sort the data into the outcomes with probabilities
 31 │         # that are larger and smaller than 1/K.
 32 │         smaller = []
 33 │         larger = []
 34 │         for kk, prob in enumerate(probs):
 35 │             self.prob[kk] = K*prob
 36 │             if self.prob[kk] < 1.0:
 37 │                 smaller.append(kk)
 38 │             else:
 39 │                 larger.append(kk)
 40 │ 
 41 │         # Loop though and create little binary mixtures that
 42 │         # appropriately allocate the larger outcomes over the
 43 │         # overall uniform mixture.
 44 │         while len(smaller) > 0 and len(larger) > 0:
 45 │             small = smaller.pop()
 46 │             large = larger.pop()
 47 │ 
 48 │             self.alias[small] = large
 49 │             self.prob[large] = (self.prob[large] - 1.0) + self.prob[small]
 50 │ 
 51 │             if self.prob[large] < 1.0:
 52 │                 smaller.append(large)
 53 │             else:
 54 │                 larger.append(large)
 55 │ 
 56 │         for last_one in smaller+larger:
 57 │             self.prob[last_one] = 1
 58 │ 
 59 │     def cuda(self):
 60 │         """Generate CUDA version of self, for GPU-based sampling."""
 61 │         self.prob = self.prob.to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
 62 │         self.alias = self.alias.to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"))
 63 │ 
 64 │     def draw(self, N):
 65 │         """
 66 │         Draw N samples from multinomial dkstribution, based on given probability array.
 67 │         
 68 │         :param N: number of samples
 69 │         :return: samples
 70 │         """
 71 │         K = self.alias.size(0)
 72 │ 
 73 │         kk = torch.zeros(N, dtype=torch.long,
 74 │                          device=self.prob.device).random_(0, K)
 75 │         prob = self.prob.index_select(0, kk)
 76 │         alias = self.alias.index_select(0, kk)
 77 │         # b is whether a random number is greater than q
 78 │         b = torch.bernoulli(prob)
 79 │         oq = kk.mul(b.long())
 80 │         oj = alias.mul((1-b).long())
 81 │ 
 82 │         return oq + oj
 83 │ 
 84 │ 
 85 │ class NCEAverage(nn.Module):
 86 │     """Implements NCEAverage Loss Function."""
 87 │     
 88 │     def __init__(self, inputSize, outputSize, K, T=0.07, momentum=0.5, use_softmax=False):
 89 │         """Instantiate NCEAverage Loss Function.
 90 │ 
 91 │         Args:
 92 │             inputSize (int): Input Size
 93 │             outputSize (int): Output Size
 94 │             K (float): K Value. See paper for more.
 95 │             T (float, optional): T Value. See paper for more. Defaults to 0.07.
 96 │             momentum (float, optional): Momentum for NCEAverage Loss. Defaults to 0.5.
 97 │             use_softmax (bool, optional): Whether to use softmax or not. Defaults to False.
 98 │         """
 99 │         super(NCEAverage, self).__init__()
100 │         self.nLem = outputSize
101 │         self.unigrams = torch.ones(self.nLem)
102 │         self.multinomial = AliasMethod(self.unigrams)
103 │         self.multinomial.cuda()
104 │         self.K = K
105 │         self.use_softmax = use_softmax
106 │ 
107 │         self.register_buffer('params', torch.tensor([K, T, -1, -1, momentum]))
108 │         stdv = 1. / math.sqrt(inputSize / 3)
109 │         self.register_buffer('memory_l', torch.rand(
110 │             outputSize, inputSize).mul_(2 * stdv).add_(-stdv))
111 │         self.register_buffer('memory_ab', torch.rand(
112 │             outputSize, inputSize).mul_(2 * stdv).add_(-stdv))
113 │ 
114 │     def forward(self, l, ab, y, idx=None): #pragma: no cover
115 │         """Apply NCEAverage Module.
116 │ 
117 │         Args:
118 │             l (torch.Tensor): Labels
119 │             ab (torch.Tensor): See paper for more.
120 │             y (torch.Tensor): True values.
121 │             idx (torch.Tensor, optional): See paper for more. Defaults to None.
122 │ 
123 │         Returns:
124 │             _type_: _description_
125 │         """
126 │         K = int(self.params[0].item())
127 │         T = self.params[1].item()
128 │         Z_l = self.params[2].item()
129 │         Z_ab = self.params[3].item()
130 │ 
131 │         momentum = self.params[4].item()
132 │         batchSize = l.size(0)
133 │         outputSize = self.memory_l.size(0)
134 │         inputSize = self.memory_l.size(1)
135 │ 
136 │         # score computation
137 │         if idx is None:
138 │             idx = self.multinomial.draw(
139 │                 batchSize * (self.K + 1)).view(batchSize, -1)
140 │             idx.select(1, 0).copy_(y.view(-1))
141 │         # sample
142 │         weight_l = torch.index_select(self.memory_l, 0, idx.view(-1)).detach()
143 │         weight_l = weight_l.view(batchSize, K + 1, inputSize)
144 │         out_ab = torch.bmm(weight_l, ab.view(batchSize, inputSize, 1))
145 │         # sample
146 │         weight_ab = torch.index_select(
147 │             self.memory_ab, 0, idx.view(-1)).detach()
148 │         weight_ab = weight_ab.view(batchSize, K + 1, inputSize)
149 │         out_l = torch.bmm(weight_ab, l.view(batchSize, inputSize, 1))
150 │ 
151 │         if self.use_softmax:
152 │             out_ab = torch.div(out_ab, T)
153 │             out_l = torch.div(out_l, T)
154 │             out_l = out_l.contiguous()
155 │             out_ab = out_ab.contiguous()
156 │         else:
157 │             out_ab = torch.exp(torch.div(out_ab, T))
158 │             out_l = torch.exp(torch.div(out_l, T))
159 │             # set Z_0 if haven't been set yet,
160 │             # Z_0 is used as a constant approximation of Z, to scale the probs
161 │             if Z_l < 0:
162 │                 self.params[2] = out_l.mean() * outputSize
163 │                 Z_l = self.params[2].clone().detach().item()
164 │                 print(
165 │                     "normalization constant Z_l is set to {:.1f}".format(Z_l))
166 │             if Z_ab < 0:
167 │                 self.params[3] = out_ab.mean() * outputSize
168 │                 Z_ab = self.params[3].clone().detach().item()
169 │                 print(
170 │                     "normalization constant Z_ab is set to {:.1f}".format(Z_ab))
171 │             # compute out_l, out_ab
172 │             out_l = torch.div(out_l, Z_l).contiguous()
173 │             out_ab = torch.div(out_ab, Z_ab).contiguous()
174 │ 
175 │         # # update memory
176 │         with torch.no_grad():
177 │             l_pos = torch.index_select(self.memory_l, 0, y.view(-1))
178 │             l_pos.mul_(momentum)
179 │             l_pos.add_(torch.mul(l, 1 - momentum))
180 │             l_norm = l_pos.pow(2).sum(1, keepdim=True).pow(0.5)
181 │             updated_l = l_pos.div(l_norm)
182 │             self.memory_l.index_copy_(0, y.view(-1), updated_l)
183 │ 
184 │             ab_pos = torch.index_select(self.memory_ab, 0, y.view(-1))
185 │             ab_pos.mul_(momentum)
186 │             ab_pos.add_(torch.mul(ab, 1 - momentum))
187 │             ab_norm = ab_pos.pow(2).sum(1, keepdim=True).pow(0.5)
188 │             updated_ab = ab_pos.div(ab_norm)
189 │             self.memory_ab.index_copy_(0, y.view(-1), updated_ab)
190 │ 
191 │         return out_l, out_ab
192 │ 
193 │ 
194 │ class NCECriterion(nn.Module):
195 │     """
196 │     Implements NCECriterion Loss.
197 │     
198 │     Eq. (12): L_{NCE}
199 │     """
200 │ 
201 │     def __init__(self, n_data):
202 │         """Instantiate NCECriterion Loss."""
203 │         super(NCECriterion, self).__init__()
204 │         self.n_data = n_data
205 │ 
206 │     def forward(self, x):
207 │         """Apply NCECriterion to Tensor Input.
208 │ 
209 │         Args:
210 │             x (torch.Tensor): Tensor Input
211 │ 
212 │         Returns:
213 │             torch.Tensor: Loss
214 │         """
215 │         bsz = x.shape[0]
216 │         m = x.size(1) - 1
217 │ 
218 │         # noise distribution
219 │         Pn = 1 / float(self.n_data)
220 │ 
221 │         # loss for positive pair
222 │         P_pos = x.select(1, 0)
223 │         log_D1 = torch.div(P_pos, P_pos.add(m * Pn + eps)).log_()
224 │ 
225 │         # loss for K negative pair
226 │         P_neg = x.narrow(1, 1, m)
227 │         log_D0 = torch.div(P_neg.clone().fill_(m * Pn),
228 │                            P_neg.add(m * Pn + eps)).log_()
229 │ 
230 │         loss = - (log_D1.sum(0) + log_D0.view(-1, 1).sum(0)) / bsz
231 │ 
232 │         return loss
233 │ 
234 │ 
235 │ class NCESoftmaxLoss(nn.Module):
236 │     """Implements Softmax cross-entropy loss (a.k.a., info-NCE loss in CPC paper)."""
237 │ 
238 │     def __init__(self):
239 │         """Instantiate NCESoftmaxLoss Module."""
240 │         super(NCESoftmaxLoss, self).__init__()
241 │         self.criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
242 │ 
243 │     def forward(self, x):
244 │         """Apply NCESoftmaxLoss to Layer Input.
245 │ 
246 │         Args:
247 │             x (torch.Tensor): Layer Input
248 │ 
249 │         Returns:
250 │             torch.Tensor: Layer Output
251 │         """
252 │         bsz = x.shape[0]
253 │         x = x.squeeze()
254 │         label = torch.zeros([bsz]).to(torch.device("cuda:0" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")).long()
255 │         loss = self.criterion(x, label)
256 │         return loss
257 │ 
258 │ 
259 │ class MultiSimilarityLoss(nn.Module):
260 │     """Implements MultiSimilarityLoss."""
261 │     
262 │     def __init__(self,):
263 │         """Initialize MultiSimilarityLoss Module."""
264 │         super(MultiSimilarityLoss, self).__init__()
265 │         self.thresh = 0.5
266 │         self.margin = 0.1
267 │ 
268 │         self.scale_pos = 5e-2
269 │         self.scale_neg = 2e-3
270 │ 
271 │     def forward(self, feats, labels):
272 │         """Apply MultiSimilarityLoss to Tensor Inputs.
273 │ 
274 │         Args:
275 │             feats (torch.Tensor): Features
276 │             labels (torch.Tensor): Labels
277 │ 
278 │         Returns:
279 │             torch.Tensor: Loss output.
280 │         """
281 │         assert feats.size(0) == labels.size(0), \
282 │             f"feats.size(0): {feats.size(0)} is not equal to labels.size(0): {labels.size(0)}"
283 │         batch_size = feats.size(0)
284 │         sim_mat = torch.matmul(feats, torch.t(feats))
285 │ 
286 │         epsilon = 1e-5
287 │         loss = list()
288 │         total = 0
289 │ 
290 │         for i in range(batch_size):
291 │             for k in range(labels[i].size(0)):
292 │                 if labels[i][k] == 1:
293 │                     total += 1
294 │                     pos_pair_ = sim_mat[i][labels[:, k] == 1]
295 │                     pos_pair_ = pos_pair_[pos_pair_ < 1 - epsilon]
296 │                     neg_pair_ = sim_mat[i][labels[:, k] == 0]
297 │ 
298 │                     if pos_pair_.size(0) == 0:
299 │                         neg_pair = neg_pair_
300 │                     else:
301 │                         neg_pair = neg_pair_[neg_pair_ +
302 │                                              self.margin > min(pos_pair_)]
303 │                     pos_pair = pos_pair_[pos_pair_ -
304 │                                          self.margin < max(neg_pair_)]
305 │ 
306 │                     if len(neg_pair) < 1 or len(pos_pair) < 1:
307 │                         continue
308 │                     
309 │                     # weighting step
310 │                     pos_loss = 1.0 / self.scale_pos * torch.log(
311 │                         1 + torch.sum(torch.exp(-self.scale_pos * (pos_pair - self.thresh))))
312 │                     neg_loss = 1.0 / self.scale_neg * torch.log(
313 │                         1 + torch.sum(torch.exp(self.scale_neg * (neg_pair - self.thresh))))
314 │                     loss.append(pos_loss + neg_loss)
315 │                     assert math.isinf(pos_loss) == False
316 │                     assert math.isinf(neg_loss) == False
317 │         if len(loss) == 0:
318 │             return torch.zeros([], requires_grad=True)
319 │ 
320 │         loss = sum(loss) / total
321 │         return loss
```
